response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced polyglot software engineer with a strong background in web, micro-services, and product engineering, but he critically fails the minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ experience — his resume shows only a 6-month C++ role at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project. The role demands deep systems-level and cloud compute platform expertise, whereas his career is overwhelmingly concentrated in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web and product contexts. There is no evidence of formal technical leadership, cloud infrastructure ownership, or large-scale compute/storage architecture work at the level this role requires.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed micro-services at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s), showing relevant systems-scale thinking
Consistent track record of knowledge-sharing through workshops, dojos, and talks, indicating mentorship inclination
Broad polyglot background and history of picking up new technologies quickly across embedded, web, and mobile domains
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project) against the 8-year minimum requirement — the single most disqualifying gap
No cloud compute platform or infrastructure ownership; AWS CDK usage at Shopify is incidental to product work, not foundational infrastructure leadership
No formal technical lead or staff-level architecture role evident; leadership experience is limited to community workshops and ad-hoc mentoring outside official job scope
